3.happening: Count the {len(values)} array slots that describe the tree.
3.why: We need the size before building node objects.
3.invariant: n is the length of the description array.

4.happening: Start an empty list of real tree nodes.
4.why: We build node objects the visualization can draw.
4.invariant: nodes holds every non-null value as a node.

5.happening: Start a lookup from node id to node object.
5.why: The recursion fetches nodes by id in O(1).
5.invariant: by_id[i] is the node stored at slot i.

6.why: It records each node as unseen, on-stack, done, or invalid — that drives the colors.

7.happening: Loop over every slot to initialize its state.
7.invariant: Slots below i already have a state.

9.happening: Start the child-slot walker just past the root.
9.why: Children are handed out in array order from slot 1.
9.invariant: child is the next unassigned slot.

10.happening: Loop over every slot to build the nodes.
10.why: Each real value becomes a node linked to its children.
10.invariant: Slots below i are already built.

11.why: A null slot is a placeholder, not a node.

12.happening: Create node {i} with value {values[i]} and empty child links.
12.why: Children are filled in from the following slots.
12.invariant: A fresh node starts with no children.

13.happening: Is there a slot left to be the left child?
13.why: Children come from the next slots in order.
13.invariant: child points at the candidate left child.

14.happening: Is that candidate slot a real node (not null)?
14.invariant: A null candidate leaves the left child empty.

15.happening: Link slot {child} as node {i}'s left child.
15.why: It is the next real node in level order.
15.invariant: left now points at a real child id.

16.why: Each slot is claimed exactly once.

17.happening: Is there a slot left to be the right child?
17.why: The right child is the slot after the left.
17.invariant: child points at the candidate right child.

18.happening: Is that candidate slot a real node (not null)?
18.invariant: A null candidate leaves the right child empty.

19.happening: Link slot {child} as node {i}'s right child.
19.why: It is the next real node in level order.
19.invariant: right now points at a real child id.

20.why: Each slot is claimed exactly once.

21.happening: Add the finished node to the tree.
21.why: It now has its value and child links.
21.invariant: nodes grows by one built node.

22.happening: Register node {i} under its id for fast lookup.
22.why: The recursion fetches nodes by id.
22.invariant: by_id maps every id to its node.

23.happening: The root is slot 0 (or nothing if the tree is empty).
23.invariant: root identifies where the recursion begins.

24.happening: Validate the whole tree from the root.
24.why: Each node is checked against its subtrees' extremes.
24.invariant: answer becomes True only if every node passes.

25.happening: Return whether the tree is a valid BST: {answer}.
25.why: No node's subtree scan found a violation.

28.happening: An absent child — nothing to check.
28.why: An empty subtree is a valid BST.
We check its subtrees' extremes against it.
28.invariant: This node is about to be tested.

29.happening: Return True for the missing child.
29.why: An empty subtree can't break the rule.

30.happening: Mark node {node_id} active — its frame is on the call stack.
30.why: Amber marks the node currently being validated.

31.why: Its subtrees' extremes are compared against it.

32.happening: Get this node's left child id.
32.why: We may need to scan the left subtree.
32.invariant: left_id is a real id or None.

33.happening: Get this node's right child id.
33.why: We may need to scan the right subtree.
33.invariant: right_id is a real id or None.

34.happening: There is a left subtree — scan it.
34.why: Its largest value must stay below this node.
An empty side can't violate the rule.

35.happening: Scan the entire left subtree for its maximum.
35.why: The biggest left value decides the left-side rule.
35.invariant: This full scan is the O(n^2) cost.

36.happening: The left max ({left_max}) is not below the node — rule broken.
36.why: Left subtree values must be strictly smaller.

36.happening: The left max ({left_max}) is safely below the node.
36.why: Every left value is strictly smaller.

37.why: Its left subtree contains too large a value.
37.invariant: A red node is why the tree fails.

38.why: A single violation rejects the whole tree.

39.happening: There is a right subtree — scan it.
39.why: Its smallest value must stay above this node.
An empty side can't violate the rule.

40.happening: Scan the entire right subtree for its minimum.
40.why: The smallest right value decides the right-side rule.
40.invariant: Another full scan on top of the recursion.

41.happening: The right min ({right_min}) is not above the node — rule broken.
41.why: Right subtree values must be strictly larger.

41.happening: The right min ({right_min}) is safely above the node.
41.why: Every right value is strictly larger.

42.why: Its right subtree contains too small a value.
42.invariant: A red node is why the tree fails.

43.why: A single violation rejects the whole tree.

44.why: Every subtree must also be a BST on its own.

45.why: The right subtree must also be a BST.

46.happening: This node holds only if both subtrees held.
46.why: A BST needs every subtree valid.

47.why: It and its subtrees obey the rule.

51.happening: A real node — include it in the max.
51.why: Its value competes to be the maximum.

52.happening: Return a value smaller than any real node.
52.why: An empty side never wins the max.
52.invariant: The sentinel keeps the max correct.

53.happening: Start the max at this node's value.
53.why: A node is at least as big as itself.
53.invariant: best is the largest seen so far.

54.happening: Find the largest value in the left subtree.
54.why: The left side may hold a bigger value.
54.invariant: left_max is the left subtree's maximum.

55.happening: The left subtree has a bigger value ({left_max}).
55.why: It should become the running maximum.

55.happening: The left subtree's max ({left_max}) is not bigger.

56.why: It is the biggest value seen so far.

57.happening: Find the largest value in the right subtree.
57.why: The right side may hold an even bigger value.
57.invariant: right_max is the right subtree's maximum.

58.happening: The right subtree has a bigger value ({right_max}).
58.why: It should become the running maximum.

58.happening: The right subtree's max ({right_max}) is not bigger.

59.why: It is the biggest value seen so far.

60.why: The caller compares it against the parent node.
60.invariant: best is this subtree's largest value.

63.happening: A real node — include it in the min.
63.why: Its value competes to be the minimum.

64.happening: Return a value larger than any real node.
64.why: An empty side never wins the min.
64.invariant: The sentinel keeps the min correct.

65.happening: Start the min at this node's value.
65.why: A node is at most as small as itself.
65.invariant: best is the smallest seen so far.

66.happening: Find the smallest value in the left subtree.
66.why: The left side may hold a smaller value.
66.invariant: left_min is the left subtree's minimum.

67.happening: The left subtree has a smaller value ({left_min}).
67.why: It should become the running minimum.

67.happening: The left subtree's min ({left_min}) is not smaller.

68.why: It is the smallest value seen so far.

69.happening: Find the smallest value in the right subtree.
69.why: The right side may hold an even smaller value.
69.invariant: right_min is the right subtree's minimum.

70.happening: The right subtree has a smaller value ({right_min}).
70.why: It should become the running minimum.

70.happening: The right subtree's min ({right_min}) is not smaller.

71.why: It is the smallest value seen so far.

72.why: The caller compares it against the parent node.
72.invariant: best is this subtree's smallest value.

init.happening: Building the tree from the level-order array.
init.why: We need node objects and links before validating.
init.invariant: Nodes pop in and connect to their parents.

recurse.happening: Recursing into a subtree — to validate it or to scan it.
recurse.why: Both the checks and the min/max scans recurse.
recurse.invariant: The call stack traces the current work.

return.why: Every node was checked against its subtree extremes.

check.happening: Testing a node or a scan comparison.
check.why: Subtree extremes decide whether the BST rule holds.

move.happening: Returning a value or verdict up the call stack.
move.why: Scans return extremes; validation returns a verdict.

update.happening: Recording state or updating a running extreme.
update.why: Colors track validation; best tracks the scan.
